approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 treats hedge accounting for recognised items exposed to foreign currency risk as a canonical hedge model, and an intragroup monetary receivable can remain exposed in consolidated financial statements under IAS 21 and IFRS 9.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 identifies cash flow hedging as a separate top-level hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk exposures, including transaction-related exposures addressed in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Net investment hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 establish net investment hedging as a distinct top-level foreign currency hedge accounting model for exposures arising from investments in foreign operations.
